gdscript_logic.evidence: Built 4 autoloads, player controller, sprite system, sim scene generator, block interaction system with signals and Area2D detection. State machine integration working after early-return bug fix.
failure_patterns: Autoload names not resolvable at parse time — use get_node('/root/Name') at runtime
Unused parameters trigger warnings treated as errors — prefix with underscore
State machine overwrite: mid-frame state change gets clobbered by later code in same _physics_process — must early-return
rules_learned: Autoload load order matters — Constants before GameState before SaveManager
Type inference from Variant triggers error — use explicit types or avoid := with Variant expressions
Early-return after state transitions in _physics_process — or downstream movement code will overwrite the new state

godot_scene_structure.evidence: Built working sim scene tree: Sim (Node2D) > generated StaticBody2D floors + Player2D (CharacterBody2D) > Sprite (Node2D) + CollisionShape2D. Camera2D as sibling to player, not child.
failure_patterns: Camera2D as child of player gets flipped/rotated with visual transforms — keep as sibling
rules_learned: Separate physics body from visual node — CharacterBody2D owns collision, child Node2D owns drawing
Use z_index on visual nodes to layer above dynamically-added floor backgrounds
Generate floor nodes in _ready() rather than hand-placing in .tscn for 10+ floors
godot_scene_structure.needs_from_human: Still want confirmation on preferred structure for modules/NPCs within floors

physics_feel.evidence: Basic movement works: run, jump, gravity, drop-through. User flagged floatiness — fixed with instant stop. Jump height required manual calc (v²/2g) to clear floor spacing. Landing squash tween adds weight. Still missing wall slide, wall jump, charged jump, sprint.
failure_patterns: move_toward() for horizontal deceleration feels floaty — use velocity.x = 0 for snappy stop
Jump velocity must be calculated against floor height: peak = v²/(2g), must exceed FLOOR_HEIGHT (96px)
Solid platforms block upward jumps — need one_way_collision = true on all floor CollisionShape2Ds
rules_learned: Browser per-frame values to Godot per-second: multiply by 60 for velocity, 3600 for acceleration, then scale by block size ratio (64/300)
One-way collision + separate collision layers enables drop-through: toggle mask bit off for 0.2s
Landing squash via tween on sprite scale (1.15, 0.85) → (1, 1) over 0.1s adds weight cheaply
physics_feel.needs_from_human: Side-by-side playtest: browser vs Godot movement. What feels wrong? User said 'not anchored in physics' and 'losing optimism in Godot'.

visual_style.evidence: Built _draw() construction worker with hardhat, vest, stripes, pants, boots. User validated the look ('fun!'). Kneeling hammer poses iterated to satisfaction — user asked for more overhead reach and extended arm, both applied successfully. Discrete pose approach confirmed as correct pattern.
failure_patterns: Sin-wave limb interpolation looks floaty/uncanny — use discrete frame poses like real pixel art
draw_rect() doesn't handle negative width — must mirror x-coordinates explicitly
scale.x on CharacterBody2D flips Camera2D children — flip must be on a child visual node only
draw_set_transform() for flipping works but is fragile with nested transforms — child Node2D scale.x = -1 is simpler and proven
rules_learned: Pixel art walk cycles: 4 discrete poses (idle, step_a, idle, step_b) at fixed timer intervals (~0.12s)
Visual flip pattern: child Node2D with scale.x = -1/1, parent CharacterBody2D never scaled
Draw body parts in layered order: back arm → legs → torso → head → front arm
z_index on sprite node to render above dynamically-created floor ColorRects
visual_style.needs_from_human: Screenshot set from browser version at key altitudes + control room

narrative_design.evidence: Extensive design docs exist. Three-line pattern is well documented.
rules_learned: Gene dialogue: if it's memorable on first read, it's too heavy. Rewrite flatter.
Suits say 'the work', builders say 'the job' — always.

audio_design.evidence: AudioManager autoload created with fade in/out and bus routing, but no audio assets exist yet.
audio_design.needs_from_human: Reference recordings of browser SFX. Godot audio bus layout (Music, SFX, UI?).

economy_systems.evidence: Food chain, happiness, bulldozer unlock all documented in detail. GameState has credits, satisfaction, food_chain_complete, builder_happiness fields.

reckoning_mechanics.evidence: Fully implemented in browser. AI behavior, scoring, flood all documented.

keeper_llm_integration.evidence: System prompt builder and BYOK connection documented. keeper.js exists as reference.

parallax_exterior.evidence: Failed with ParallaxBackground (3 iterations), succeeded with manual Node2D offsets. Sky/star shaders working. City layers with per-layer ground planes. Floor backgrounds segmented to leave windows transparent. Horizontal parallax added — same pattern as vertical, user-approved.
failure_patterns: ParallaxBackground interacts with zoom — use plain Node2Ds with manual offset
Full-width floor bg blocks parallax through windows — segment bg to skip window columns
Single shared ground plane drifts from parallax city layers — each layer needs own ground
GL Compatibility rejects 'return' in fragment shaders — use multiplicative masking
const vec3 outside functions may not compile — declare inside fragment()
rules_learned: Manual parallax: layer.y = cam_y * (1.0 - depth_factor), layer.x = cam_dx * factor — zoom-independent, works for both axes
canvas_item shaders for sky/stars — pass camera_y as uniform from _process()
City layers fade via modulate.a driven by smoothstep on altitude
Each city _draw() layer draws its own ground rect for visual anchoring
Camera limits (limit_left/right) replace thick walls for view containment
parallax_exterior.needs_from_human: Visual feedback — does the parallax look good? Do altitude transitions feel right? Are buildings positioned naturally?

block_interaction.evidence: Built full claim system: block.gd with Area2D detection, 8-strike hammer sequence, border Line2D growth, spark/completion particles, screen shake, GameState persistence. Iterative pose tuning workflow validated with user.
failure_patterns: State machine overwrite bug — see f012 and rules/godot_state_machine.md
rules_learned: Block nodes replace ColorRects for interactive elements — same visual, but with Area2D and state
Area2D collision_mask must match player collision_layer for body_entered to fire
Rapid strike animations (8 x 0.075s) feel more physical than slow directional poses (4 x 0.45s)
User prefers kneeling single-spot hammering over directional strikes around the block perimeter

canvas2d_to_godot_porting.evidence: Established _draw() as Canvas2D replacement. Block grid rendering, floor generation, character drawing, and city skyline layers all working. Parallax implemented with shaders.
failure_patterns: Canvas save/restore → Godot has no equivalent — use draw_set_transform() or child nodes with transforms
rules_learned: _draw() on Node2D replaces Canvas2D fillRect/strokeRect patterns
ColorRect nodes for static backgrounds, _draw() for animated/conditional rendering
Collision layers replace browser's manual overlap checking
canvas2d_to_godot_porting.needs_from_human: Establish porting patterns for: parallax scrolling, module animated art, camera shake
